target.description: Adds 'Axiom Surfacing' subsection to the Response Contract: prefer restating governing premises over operating in unstated ambiguity; bounded by signal-to-noise.

routing_deviation.reason: Owner directed Claude Code to clear both review-debt items in this session.
routing_deviation.mitigation: No blocking findings; change is a bounded operator calibration. Low risk from routing deviation.

reverified_gates.evidence: blocking_count: 0, warning_count: 2 (env/path — same run as peer-review-protocol review above)
1 file changed, 11 insertions. Scope confirmed: docs/AURORA_INTERACTION_WARRANT_POLICY_v1.md only. New §'Axiom Surfacing' under Response Contract.

reverified_gates.command: manual diff read against AURORA_INTERACTION_WARRANT_POLICY_v1.md §Response Contract
reverified_gates.evidence: Addition slots cleanly between 'Recommend the next action with uncertainty labeled' and 'Collaborative Expansion Rule'. No existing section contradicted. Anti-sycophancy safeguards remain intact.

findings.evidence: 'Restate only while each clarification reduces ambiguity more than it adds overhead' — no testable rule; judgment-dependent.
findings.description: The signal-to-noise bound is a judgment call, which is appropriate for a policy document. The §Calibration section already accounts for this via false-positive/false-negative tracking and obstruction rate metrics. The combination is coherent — the policy sets the intent; calibration measures whether the judgment is being applied well in practice.

verdict_rationale: The Axiom Surfacing addition is a well-bounded operator calibration. It addresses a specific failure mode (assistant operates on premises the user has not seen) without creating a verbosity mandate — the signal-to-noise bound and the 'cut recursion once the conclusion is fixed' clause prevent misapplication. The addition is coherent with the existing Response Contract and does not contradict any anti-sycophancy or anti-contrarianism safeguard. F1 is waived: the subjectivity of the signal-to-noise judgment is by design and handled by the §Calibration feedback loop.
